engine_apf_state_dev_side: DISABLED — Genspark answered naturally without intercept (text-only branch confirmed)
prompt_submitted: What is the result of one plus one?
observed_user_visible_response: Simple text bubble: '1 더하기 1은 **2**입니다. ✨' (NO project card UI, NO tool-call). Below the answer Genspark also rendered four follow-up suggestion chips ('Explain why 1 + 1 equals 2 in simple terms', 'Show me 5 more basic addition examples with answers', 'Create a beginner-friendly worksheet with 10 simple addition problems', 'Compare addition and subtraction in a simple table for a beginner').
page_url_after_submit: https://www.genspark.ai/agents?id=2d768c46-7a8d-4478-a1b6-efbd0aec5b2e (still routes through project page even for plain-text reply)
notes: PLAIN-TEXT BRANCH SCHEMA captured — directly complements iter13 (#1031, tool-call branch). (1) ENDPOINT: POST https://www.genspark.ai/api/agent/ask_proxy HTTP/2, status=200, content-type=text/event-stream, body_size=7,555 bytes, total elapsed 5,257.53 ms. ~4x smaller and ~7x faster than #1031 (29,489 B / 35,240 ms). (2) EVENT COUNT: 21 (vs 102 for tool-call). (3) FULL TYPE SEQUENCE (in order, all 21): [project_start, message_field(_updatetime), message_start, project_field(name='Result of one plus one'), message_field_delta(content='1'), message_field_delta(content=' 더'), message_field_delta(content='하기'), message_field_delta(content=' '), message_field_delta(content='1'), message_field_delta(content='은'), message_field_delta(content=' **'), message_field_delta(content='2'), message_field_delta(content='**'), message_field_delta(content='입니다'), message_field_delta(content='.'), message_field_delta(content=' ✨'), message_field(content='1 더하기 1은 **2**입니다. ✨'), message_field(reasoning_id='rs_...'), message_field(reasoning_encrypted_content='gAAAA...'), message_result(finish='stop'), project_field(status='FINISHED')]. (4) TYPE COUNTS: message_field_delta=12 (all field_name='content'), message_field=4, project_field=2, project_start=1, message_start=1, message_result=1. (5) CONTENT PATH CONFIRMED: field_name='content' deltas (12 chunks, ~2 bytes each, mostly Korean tokenizer pieces) concatenate to the full visible message '1 더하기 1은 **2**입니ds. ✨' (20 bytes UTF-8). This REFUTES iter7-11 (project-style SSE with nested choices[].delta.content) and CONFIRMS #1031's hypothesis #7 about the content delta path. (6) TERMINATOR: same as #1031 — `message_result` carries the full assembled `message.content` plus `message.session_state._finish_reason='stop'` and `_llm_usage` ({prompt_tokens:22228, completion_tokens:79, total_tokens:22307, reasoning_tokens:60}). NO explicit `*_end` / `[DONE]` sentinel. (7) DIFFERENCES vs #1031 (tool-call): (a) tool_calls is null (vs 1-element array carrying ask_user_questions in #1031), (b) only 1 message_result vs 3 in #1031 (the tool-call branch emits 3 message_results due to multi-stage response), (c) message_field events emit a non-delta `field_name='content'` AFTER the deltas to finalize the field (event #16), (d) trailing `project_field(field_name='status', field_value='FINISHED')` AFTER message_result — this is the project-level completion marker, present in plain-text path but project_start/project_field carry minimal data (just name and status) vs #1031 where project was auto-titled '오늘 날씨 문의'. (8) MINIMUM ENVELOPE FOR PLAIN-TEXT WARNING (refined from #1031 hypothesis): [project_start, message_start(role=assistant), message_field_delta(field_name='content', delta=<warning chunk(s)>)*, message_field(field_name='content', field_value=<full warning>), message_result(message.content=<full warning>, session_state._finish_reason='stop'), project_field(field_name='status', field_value='FINISHED')]. Minimum viable count = 5-6 events for a warning. (9) ID SCHEME: project_id and message_id are DISTINCT uuids (in #1043: project=2d768c46..., message=2e63d7ec...); project_start carries only `id` (=project uuid), message_start carries `message_id` + `project_id` linkage. (10) _event_index field is monotonic int 0..20. (11) IMPORTANT: even plain-text replies route through /agents?id=<proj-uuid> page — frontend always wraps responses in a project context regardless of content kind. Full sanitized schema dump in files/1043/extract_1043.json; raw HAR (gitignored) in files/1043/genspark_plain_1043.har.

schema_summary_plain_text_branch.terminator: message_result (no explicit *_end or [DONE]); trailing project_field(status=FINISHED) marks project completion

comparison_vs_1031_tool_call_branch.scaling: plain-text branch is ~5x fewer events, ~4x smaller body, ~7x faster, and uses a SINGLE message_result (vs 3 in tool-call branch). Both branches share identical envelope skeleton (project_start → message_start → message_field_delta* → message_result) — the only structural difference is which delta field_name carries the payload and whether tool_calls is populated in message.tool_calls.

id_correlation.message_id: DISTINCT uuid carried in message_start.message_id and *.message_id

key_design_inputs_for_warning_envelope_F.envelope_works_for_both_branches: YES — frontend tolerates the plain-text envelope even when no tool_calls present. Tool-call branch is a strict superset (extra message_field_delta events with field_name='tool_calls[*].function.arguments' and tool_calls populated in message_result.message.tool_calls). For APF warning injection, the plain-text envelope is sufficient and minimal.

capture_method: windows-mcp UI automation on existing logged-in Chrome (port 9222 not available, no CDP needed). DevTools opened via F12, Network tab with Preserve log + Disable cache + filter='ask_proxy' (preserved from #1031 session). Prompt typed via Type tool, submitted via Enter. After-response screenshot shows immediate completion (~5s vs ~35s for tool-call branch). HAR exported via 'Export HAR (sanitized)' button to worktree files/1043/.
